title: hackelia diffusa var. arida
intro: Hackelia diffusa var. arida, also known as sticky geranium or stiffstem beardtongue, is a perennial flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Hackelia diffusa var. arida include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
